title: GeNAS: Neural Architecture Search with Better Generalization
focus: Improving generalization of architectures found through Neural Architecture Search (NAS)

methodology.problem: Quantitative measurement of generalizability during architecture search is under-explored
methodology.approach: Finding an optimal proxy measurement to discriminate generalizable architectures
methodology.limitations: Performance-based search (PBS) methods show poor generalizability on test sets and other tasks

key_findings: Low correlation between angle measure and flatness measure on NAS-Bench-201
Architectures with fast convergence during training tend to show better generalizability
ABS estimates angle between initial and final network parameters as a proxy for generalizability
Flat loss minima can achieve low generalization error even with distribution gaps
